task: Build a Tron Mode theme extension at src/extensions/tron-mode/index.ts. This is a visual theme that transforms the globe into an NVIDIA-inspired cyberpunk aesthetic with glowing green lines on a dark background. Implementation: 1) Create the extension as kind='theme' with activate/deactivate. 2) On activate: switch to 'blank-black' base map, enable Cesium PostProcessStageLibrary bloom (scene.postProcessStages.bloom.enabled=true, set glowOnly=false, contrast=128, brightness=-0.2, delta=1.0, sigma=2.0, stepSize=1.0), add a custom edge-detection post-process stage with green color, set globe.material to an ElevationContour material with green color (Cesium.Color.fromCssColorString('#76B900') which is NVIDIA green), set contour spacing to 500m, enable globe.showWaterEffect=false, set atmosphere hue/saturation for green tint, make all existing GeoJSON layer stroke colors green (#76B900). 3) On deactivate: remove all post-process stages added, reset globe.material to null, restore base map to 'default', restore atmosphere. 4) Register commands: tron:enable and tron:disable. 5) Add to extension catalog with tags: theme, visual, nvidia, cyberpunk, neon. The NVIDIA brand green is #76B900. Use it as the primary accent. Secondary glow can be slightly brighter #8ACE00.

Add a lat/lon graticule grid overlay. Create a new extension at src/extensions/graticule/index.ts with kind='capability'. On activate, create a Cesium GridImageryProvider or use Cesium.GridMaterialProperty to draw thin latitude/longitude lines every 10 degrees on the globe surface. Lines should be subtle (rgba(255,255,255,0.15) or similar) and not interfere with other layers. Register commands graticule:show and graticule:hide. The grid should be added as an imagery layer so it respects the layer ordering system. If GridImageryProvider is not available, create a custom UrlTemplateImageryProvider that generates grid tile images via canvas, or use the Cesium Grid material on the globe.

Add atmosphere glow control commands. In src/ai/core-commands.ts add a command core:set-glow with params: color (string, optional, CSS color like '#76B900' or 'cyan'), intensity (number 0-1, optional), and thickness (number 0-1, optional). The command controls the atmospheric glow ring around the globe edge by setting viewer.scene.atmosphere.hueShift (map the color hue to Cesium's -0.5 to 0.5 range), saturationShift (from intensity), and brightnessShift (from thickness). Also expose viewer.scene.skyAtmosphere.hueShift/saturationShift/brightnessShift for the sky portion. Add a second command core:reset-glow that restores all atmosphere values to Cesium defaults (all shifts = 0). Follow existing command patterns.

Add a wireframe globe mode to the Tron Mode extension (src/extensions/tron-mode/index.ts). When Tron mode activates, make all GeoJSON boundary/coastline layers render as outline-only with no fill. Set polygon fill to transparent (Cesium.Color.TRANSPARENT) while keeping stroke visible. Also add a separate standalone command core:wireframe in src/ai/core-commands.ts that toggles wireframe mode independently: it sets all GeoJSON polygon fills to transparent and all polyline/polygon outlines to a specified color (default white). The command takes an optional color param. This gives a clean outline-only look for any base map, not just Tron mode.

Add a screenshot/presentation mode. Create a new command core:presentation-mode in src/ai/core-commands.ts that hides all UI chrome (sidebar, toolbar, app dock, chat panel, status strip, bottom bar, global controls) leaving only the bare globe visible. Pressing Escape or running the command again restores the UI. Implementation: toggle a CSS class on the root app container that sets display:none on all UI overlay elements. Store the state in a module-level boolean. Also add a keyboard shortcut: pressing F11 or P (with no modifiers, not in text input) toggles presentation mode. Add the key handler in CesiumViewer.tsx setupKeyboard().
